items.park_reason: New evidence (2026-06-01): m2c-shape adoption [var_v0 accumulator + nested if (rect[2]!=0) { if (rect[3]==0) return -1; ...body...; return call(); } + outer return -1;] reduces honest cheat-free distance 21 -> 7 (build_insns 49=49). Defeats cross-jump merge by mixing exit forms (TWO inline return -1 + one tail return-via-call) so jump2 cannot merge the early-return suffixes. Verified: bnez->body / j end / li v0,-1 split form emerges; sll v0,s1,16 fills bnez delay slot via reorg. The residual 7 diffs are PURE register-allocation choices the cc1 local_alloc makes: target lw a0,0(s0) for arg0[0] vs sandbox lw v0,0(s0); cascades to sw a0,0(a1) vs sw v0,0(a1). lreg dump (tmp/b6c8.i.lreg) shows pseudo 94 (arg0[0] temp) is a local allocno that local_alloc picks  for because  is freed by the BF28 store scheduled EARLY. Target keeps packed live in  across the load via LATE BF28 store scheduling (delays sw v0,(BF28) past arg0[0] load). Mechanism: GCC 2.7.2 sched2 priority for the BF28 store depends on its downstream dep chain depth (0 hops in cc1's natural form) - to delay it past arg0[0] requires either (a) extending the BF28 store's anti-dep chain so it cannot fire early, or (b) preventing local_alloc from picking  for pseudo 94. Adding any function-scope local (s32 packed; s32 a0_val; etc) breaks the early-return split (regresses to 21) due to frame-shift impact on jump2. NEXT untried levers: (1) declare bf24 as a callee-save register-class hinted pseudo to lengthen its live range so it cannot share with arg0[0]; (2) write arg0[0] read as a chained store-then-load through bf24+offset alias so the load-store dependency chain explicitly extends BF28's store priority; (3) probe BB2_SCHED_DEBUG/BB2_PRIO_DEBUG instrumented cc1 dump of THIS function (tmp/gccdbg/cc1 SHA1 a17fc6bbcb... May 30) to see exact INSN_PRIORITY of BF28 store vs arg0[0] load in the new structure; (4) restructure the call to take arg0[0] as a register-promoted arg (e.g. pass through a wrapper) that forces a0 allocation upstream of the body. The 19-rule cluster remains entirely register-renames after m2c-shape adoption; if any of these levers brings cc1 to pick  for arg0[0] naturally, all 19 rules collapse together.

Session 2 (2026-06-01) re-park: confirmed floor 6 via Lever B (named-intermediate u32 mask). 26+ new structural levers tested this session (goto-end+ret_val staging variants, named intermediate addr/mask ordering swaps, type casts void*/u8*/u32-hex-shifts, dispatcher fn-ptr forms) — none cleanly below 6. NEW CHEAT-FORM identified: function-pointer return-type lie u32 *(*disp)(...) + (void)result; capture (sandbox 0, retire SHA1==oracle) — rejected per [[no-new-park-categories]] cheat-form vetting checklist (semantic type mismatch with no programmer purpose, lies to GCC about a void-returning fn call to manipulate \ liveness/scheduling — same intent as forbidden Lever D). Documented as forbidden in memory/project/func-8007b844-conditional-dead-store.md session-2 log. Concrete next hypothesis: only remaining un-tried lever class is a *real* semantically-meaningful operation after the AND that consumes \ — but target only has one store, so no such operation exists. Recommendation for next session: surface for user policy on canonical-asm authorization (scan_hand_coded still LOW 0/8, but two evidence-driven C-exhaustion sessions may warrant policy discussion).

ASM-SUSPECT/structural — 298-insn gpu_PutDispEnv-style display environment setup (cached-diff + GP1 packet sends via g_gpu_dev_table[0x10]). Asmfix rule replace_with_asmfile bridges. Partial pure-C reconstruction from m2c brings sandbox 296 -> 132 (286/298 insns). Remaining 132-distance gap: 6 missing lhu+sll/sra cast-pattern pairs (GCC combine folds (s32)(s16)u16var to lh; needs structural lever to defeat fold), plus scheduling/register-allocation cascade through the 4-comparison + video-mode + GP1 packet build region. Multi-session pure-C work; needs same depth as defeat-licm-hoist-var-reuse class fixes. Oracle SHA1 green throughout (asmfix continues to provide target bytes).

arg1-preload lever found this session pushes honest floor 20 -> 18 (lower than prior session 19-22). See memory/project/func-8007c7a0-arg1-preload-lever.md for evidence. Structural ceiling: missing 'move a3,v0' park-merge insn that GCC won't emit without simultaneously-live pseudos. Next: examine matched sibling func_8007C748 + try declaration-order swap a la param-local-alias-prologue-pair-flip.

RA-coupling plateau (same cluster as sibling func_8007C7A0): 21 rules incl 19 register-rename substs + 1 frame-size + 1 lost-codegen insert. This session: clean v8 form (var_a1=arg1 unconditional + strip int new_var/new_var2/dead 'if(!D_8009BE7A){}' padding) drops sandbox floor 20->17 (1 below C7A0's 18 floor). Same structural ceiling: missing target 'move a3,v0' park-merge insn at idx 16 that GCC coalesces away. NOT committable (no rule retire). Evidence: memory/project/func-8007c86c-clean-form-floor.md.

cross-jump merge DIRECTION wall: GCC merges case 1 and case 2 tails into a shared .L139 mini-block instead of target's case 2 -> case 1 backjump structure. Honest pure-C floor LOWERED 11 -> 10 (2026-06-01) via three combined levers documented in memory/project/func-8007ca00-m2c-shape-floor.md: (1) m2c-shape body adoption (single switch with shared block_5 label, default-at-end so it merges with shared tail); (2) var_a0 split (case 1 BE77!=0 pre-loads arg0[0] into var_a0 before goto block_5; case 2 BE77!=0 does the same); (3) 2-statement subtract { s32 tmp = 0x400 - var_v1; tmp -= var_a0; return tmp; } block-local form (vs single expression — controls subu order and keeps tmp in $v0). All pure-C, no cheats. Residual 10 diffs are reorg.c delay-slot-fill decisions (case 1 beqz delay should be li v0,1024 per target; case 2 BE77!=0 j delay should be li v0,1024 not sra v1,v1,1) and case 2 BE77==0 register chain ( vs ). Resume avenues (untried this session): directed permuter from new sandbox-10 base in permuter/ca00/; instrumented tmp/gccdbg/cc1 with BB2_FILL_DEBUG to find why reorg declines the li v0,1024 copy into beqz delay; rule UPDATE (the 13 existing rules are calibrated for the OLD body and can't retire on the new shape — need new rules for case 1 beqz delay fill + case 2 j delay fill + case 2 BE77==0 v0/v1 swap). NOT a documented auto-park category.

PURE-C-DRAFT-PROGRESS: sandbox 76 → 52 honest distance, build_insns 151 == target 151 EXACTLY. Structural decomp complete: 2-arg sig (arg2/arg3 unused), goto-form clamps mirroring sibling func_8007CE0C, &D_800F187C captured into local p187C to share the symbol-ref (saves 1 lui;sw pair AND makes cc1 use  matching target; also auto-grows frame to 0x40), *(u16*)arg0 & 0x3F for flag check (lhu matching target). Levers verified: arg0 in , arg1 in , 0xE3/E5 in /, both clamps in 6+9-insn shape. Residual 52 is list-scheduler INSN_PRIORITY in big-packet store sub-block (idx 50-89) and small-packet (117-137) — same coupling family as cpu_side_move_dir_4 / marionation_Exec. Draft preserved tmp/cbb0_score52_draft.c. Resume avenues (UNTRIED this session, untriable from old 149-cheat-strip-corrupted base): (1) directed permuter from clean offset-0 target at score-52 base, (2) BB2_SCHED_DEBUG + BB2_PRIO_DEBUG on score-52 source, (3) reorder big-packet path to delay 0x03FFFFFF materialization. NOT viable per policy: pad arrays, register pins, volatile coercion, dead stores, scheduling-barrier __asm__. See memory/project/func-8007cbb0-pure-c-draft.md.

Semantic frame investigation (memory/project/func-8007ce0c-semantic-investigation.md, 2026-06-01): 32-byte vars region (0x10-0x2F) in target frame=80 is entirely UNREFERENCED in func body — confirmed by reading every -relative access in asm/funcs/func_8007CE0C.s. cc1 emits frame=64 from any tested pure-C source; only forbidden patterns (s32 buf[N], (void)&local — both detected by engine/volatile_cheats) reproduce target frame size. NOT canonical-asm: scan_hand_coded tier=LOW score=1/8 (only S4); diagnose's CANONICAL-ASM verdict is false positive (mflo heuristic over-fires). Pure-C floor 41 reachable via compare-flip + drop cheat-asm pin (vs HEAD 48), but no rule retirement so not committed. Per [[no-new-park-categories]] no frame-size-infrastructure carve-out; per [[dead-vars-local-array]] no padding workaround. 23 rules remain (now drift-robust via {lbl#N} per db690e7, so this function is no longer a sibling-coupling blocker). REVISIT WHEN: new C lever discovered (e.g. via instrumented cc1 alloc/sched dumps in the BB2_*_DEBUG family), OR hand-coded-signal reassessment reveals evidence the scan_hand_coded heuristic missed.

Canonical jump-table infrastructure, not a register/codegen cheat. The 24 asmfix rules (15 renames of GCC .L labels to external jtbl_800108CC + 8 jlabel conversions + 1 delete_between stripping GCC's emitted table) are structurally required: the switch's jump table jtbl_800108CC sits in the FIRST rodata block (asm/data 800.rodata_pre.o, bb2.ld:25, addr 0x800108CC), while code6cac_b2.o's own .rodata links far later (bb2.ld:50). A GCC in-object jump table can only land in code6cac_b2's late rodata, never at 0x108CC, so referencing the external jtbl symbol + deleting GCC's table is mandatory. Zero-asmfix COMPLETED-C would need relocating the jtbl into code6cac_b2.c AND reordering the whole global rodata layout (project-wide, needs user sign-off). This is the project standard for the 26 jtbl-rename functions whose tables splat carved into asm/data rodata. Separately, sandbox cannot score this file: file-wide cheat-asm stripping shifts maspsx indices and crashes a sibling fn's index-based regfix reorder rule (KeyError in tools/regfix.py).

register-alloc coupling — arg pseudos win allocno tiebreaker for low callee-saves (creation order), forcing rotation. chain-extender already present, volatile-bump regresses (cost > saved). same mechanism as cpu_side_move_dir_4 at larger surface; full pinning in register-alloc-pure-c.md.
